assertions.text: Output contains at least one markdown table
assertions.evidence: Two full markdown tables: 'Order Cancellation' and 'Return Eligibility', both using | column | syntax

assertions.text: Table covers cancellation rules — includes rows for states where cancellation is allowed and where it is not
assertions.evidence: Table 1 rows: PENDING (yes), CONFIRMED (yes), SHIPPED (no), DELIVERED (no), CANCELLED (no) — all five states covered with both allowed and not-allowed outcomes

assertions.text: The 30-day return window rule is addressed — either as a threshold column, a separate table, or flagged as an open question
assertions.evidence: Table 2 'Return Eligibility' has a 'Return Window (Policy)' column with value '30 days', plus boundary rows at 30 and 31 days, plus Open Questions item 1 about boundary inclusivity

assertions.text: Uses value sets {PENDING, CONFIRMED} or equivalent to express 'regardless of which starting state' for cases where a rule holds across states
assertions.evidence: Table 2 row 'Return requested on non-delivered order' uses '{PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, CANCELLED}' in the Current Status cell. Summary line also states 'cancellation is allowed from {PENDING, CONFIRMED}'

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: Both tables have 'Cancellation Allowed?' and 'Return Allowed?' output columns, plus 'Reason?' in each table
